claimText: DNA profiling is primarily based on the variability present in noncoding regions of DNA called Variable Number of Tandem Repeats (VNTRs).
evidenceExcerpt: DNA profiling is based on variability in noncoding regions of DNA called Variable Number of Tandem Repeats (VNTRs).
examUse: Statement-based question identifying the specific regions of DNA used in fingerprinting.
distractorHooks: DNA profiling is based on coding regions of DNA
DNA profiling relies on regions with constant tandem repeats

claimText: Mitochondrial DNA (mtDNA) profiling is specifically used in situations where nuclear DNA is degraded.
evidenceExcerpt: Mitochondrial DNA (mtDNA) is used when nuclear DNA is degraded.
examUse: Scenario-based question on choosing the appropriate DNA profiling method for degraded forensic samples.
distractorHooks: mtDNA is used when mitochondrial DNA is degraded
mtDNA is preferred over intact nuclear DNA for standard profiling

claimText: Y chromosome profiling traces paternal lineage by focusing on Short Tandem Repeats (STRs) inherited from father to son.
evidenceExcerpt: Y chromosome profiling focuses on STRs inherited from father to son.
examUse: Correct-pair question matching Y chromosome profiling with paternal lineage tracing.
distractorHooks: Y chromosome profiling traces maternal lineage
Y chromosome STRs are inherited from mother to son

claimText: Single Nucleotide Polymorphisms (SNPs) are genetic variations consisting of single-base differences that are unique to individuals.
evidenceExcerpt: Single Nucleotide Polymorphisms (SNPs) are single-base differences unique to individuals.
examUse: Statement-based question defining SNPs in the context of genetic profiling.
distractorHooks: SNPs involve large structural changes in chromosomes
